'''Brezzic Marr''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] who served in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He was assigned as the commanding officer of the [[105th Stormtrooper Platoon]] while the unit was stationed aboard [[Starlyte Station]]. He was fanatically devoted to the Empire and his leadership kept the unit of less-than-sterling troopers from failing further. In an attempt to instill pride and creativity within his platoon, Marr broke with Stormtrooper Corps regulations by allowing the personalization of troopers' [[Stormtrooper armor/Legends|armor]].

==Biography==
Brezzic Marr's history was unknown prior to his assignment to the [[105th Stormtrooper Platoon]] however there were several rumors circulating that hinted at mental instability. He was an incredibly skilled tactician, however, and his strategies were entered into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] tactics manual. Fanatically loyal yet ultimately pragmatic, Marr has allowed his unit to break several rules while assigned to the [[Space station/Legends|space station]] [[Starlyte Station]] under [[Arissa Fawn]]. First he allows his unit to customize their armor, and secondly he allows his command to accept bribes from [[Talandro Starlyte]], owner of the station and a notorious [[black marketeer]].<ref name="WHSV" /> The 105th was well disciplined and their drills were impressive to watch which was why Marr would often have them drill in very public places, like right outside the entrance to the [[Falling Star Saloon]]<ref name="DT">"[[Dark Tremors]]"</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Mentally unstable yet fanatically loyal, Brezzic Marr presents a formidable obstacle to those wishing to go against the Empire in this remote part of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Even his superior, [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] Fawn was afraid of what Marr might do. He was extremely intense and slightly sadistic and he had a faraway look that was truly disturbing for others. Although Marr accepted bribes from Starlyte he made it clear that no amount of money would save the station owner if he ever went against the [[Palpatine/Legends|Emperor's]] will.<ref name="WHSV" />

==Skills and abilities==
Highly skilled in both combat and tactics, Marr was fearsome in combat. He was also well trained in a variety of other skills, include [[Starship/Legends|starship]] gunnery, investigation, and demolitions. Marr was tactically brilliant and a strong leader.<ref name="WHSV" />

==Equipment==
Marr heavily customized his [[Stormtrooper armor/Legends|stormtrooper armor]] and emblazoned the unit sigil, an Imperial symbol with arcing lighting behind it, on his shoulder pad. He wielded a [[T-6 heavy blaster pistol|BlasTech T-6 "Thunderer"]] [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]] and also carried a [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]], a couple of [[Vibroknife/Legends|vibroknives]] and a [[Bandolier/Legends|bandoleer]] of [[Stun grenade/Legends|stun grenades]].

==Behind the scenes==
Brezzic Marr was designed by [[Paul Danner]] and [[Bill Smith]] for ''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]'', a supplement for the ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' that was published by [[West End Games]] in [[1997]]. He was illustrated by [[Joe Corroney]].
